title: Process witness and filing phase
summary: Sixty-third structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-4 wave first promotes `tundra-short-season` into a full `Thaw Window` process outing through the existing living-world seam, then deepens the notebook return through current filing surfaces.

execution_order.why_now: The living-world seam is now mature enough for one more authored process-centered outing.
The board already hints at the thaw-fringe process; carrying that cue into the live outing is the cleanest next lane-4 gain.
The outing must stay soft and authored, not timed or punishing.
A process-centered outing wants a stronger return page to feel complete.

execution_order.goal: Give the filed `Short Season` return a `Thaw Window` page stamp.
execution_order.why_now: The outing now reads clearly in play, so the next gain is to make the current preview and notice seams feel like the same little field page.
The field-page return should deepen without becoming busy.

guardrails: Do not add a new route type unless the current Route v2 seam truly cannot carry the behavior.
Reuse existing world-state, notebook, and support surfaces.
Keep the outing soft and observational.
acceptance_focus: One process-centered outing feels distinct and worthwhile.
The notebook return feels more like field synthesis.
Lane 4 keeps deepening the outing loop without shell growth.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-4.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-process-witness-and-filing-phase.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-thaw-window-process-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-thaw-window-filing-handoff.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 4's next long-push follow-on after the high-country Route v2 wave: keep the canonical `Short Season` filed note stable, but add one compact shared `Thaw Window.` display stamp to the current note-preview and filed-notice seams so the return feels more like the same field page the outing just earned.
